stubs_used: Semgrep findings — created docs/security-audits/uat-feat-baseline-security-audit.md with Open=false; no real Semgrep run.
Stub TypeScript source/test — only `// stub` files in src/ and tests/; no Node/tsc/jest build was run.
GitHub remote — used https://example.com/fake.git via host=other (option 4); push failed (expected, no real network).
Phase advancement — manually bumped current_phase from 1->2 via jq because check-phase-gate.sh is consistency-only and does not advance phase.
WORKAROUND for upgrade-project.sh poc_mode detection bug: stubbed .claude/intake-progress.json with poc_mode=sponsored_poc so the script could proceed (see Critical bug below).
Pending-approval sentinel — wrote real sentinel via scripts/pending-approval.sh --offer ... to test whether upgrade respects it (it does not — see High bug below).
init_driver: Heredoc with canned answers fed to bash init.sh: project name 'uat-agent-80', description, platform=4 (web; alphabetical desktop=1, mcp-server=2, mobile=3, web=4), track=2 (standard), deployment=2 (organizational), governance=2 (Sponsored POC), language=7 (typescript), project_dir, Y for confirm, Y for proceed plan, host_choice=4 (other; first attempt mis-numbered triggered Invalid choice warning then accepted on retry), visibility=1 (private), https://example.com/fake.git, attest=yes. init.sh exited non-zero because create_and_protect_remote git push to fake.git failed under set -euo pipefail; project skeleton was nonetheless fully created and usable for downstream tests (same behavior as agent-1 and agent-14).
scenario_specific_notes: Sponsored POC mode wired correctly at init: phase-state.json reports poc_mode='sponsored_poc', deployment='organizational', track='standard', compliance_ready=false. init.sh emitted the warning 'POC MODE: sponsored poc — Phase 4 (production release) is blocked until you upgrade.'
Sponsored POC + Standard track was accepted (no track upgrade prompt fired because Standard is the minimum acceptable track for Sponsored POC).

Upgrade --to-production correctly removed poc_mode, set last_upgrade=2026-04-25, preserved current_phase=2, preserved track=standard, preserved deployment=organizational, preserved build_loop history (uat-feat-baseline still recorded).

Upgrade output explicitly stated 'POC constraints removed. This project is now production-ready' and prompted to review CLAUDE.md/PROJECT_INTAKE.md governance fields. Auto-committed via 'chore(upgrade): POC -> production'.
Upgrade did NOT respect the .claude/pending-approval.json sentinel — it ran through to completion without warning or refusal (see High bug below).

tests.name: init_sh_exits_zero
tests.details: init.sh exited 1 (same root cause as agent-1 / agent-14): create_and_protect_remote() git push to fake.git fails; with set -euo pipefail the script aborts after project creation but before verify-install/print_next_steps. The on-disk project is otherwise complete and usable.

framework_bugs.summary: init.sh exits non-zero on documented happy path when remote push fails
framework_bugs.reproduction: Run init.sh selecting host=other and pasting https://example.com/fake.git as the remote URL (per runbook Setup constraint). git push to fake.git fails; create_and_protect_remote returns 1; set -euo pipefail aborts main() before verify-install and print_next_steps. Exit code = 1.
framework_bugs.expected: init.sh either tolerates a remote-push failure (prints remediation guidance and finishes) or exits with a documented BLOCKED code that survives set -e gracefully.
framework_bugs.actual: Bare `[FAIL] Push failed — verify URL and credentials` then immediate non-zero exit. (Confirms agent-1 and agent-14 finding for sponsored_poc/standard/web/typescript config.)

framework_bugs.summary: init.sh has no scriptable mode for UAT — heredoc driver fragile
framework_bugs.reproduction: Pipe canned answers via stdin to bash init.sh; only --dry-run/--help flags exist. Number/order of prompts depends on installed-tool state. Host prompt fires on a separate stdin read after project creation, which mis-aligned my answer pool and produced 'Invalid choice. Enter a number between 1 and 4.' before accepting on the retry.
framework_bugs.expected: A non-interactive flag (or --answers-from FILE) so UAT and CI can drive init.sh deterministically without per-environment prompt drift.
framework_bugs.actual: Same brittleness as agents 1 and 14; the Git host prompt is particularly sensitive because it lives outside collect_project_info().

documentation_gaps.gap: init.sh advertises only --dry-run and --help; runbook documents --non-interactive/--project/--platform/--track/--scenario/--upgrade-from flags that do not exist.
documentation_gaps.where: init.sh main() flag parser vs Reports/uat-2026-04-25/RUNBOOK.md Section A example.

documentation_gaps.gap: Number/order of init.sh prompts is undocumented and varies with installed-tool state and per-platform conditional branches; the host/visibility prompts fire after project creation and are read separately.
documentation_gaps.where: init.sh collect_project_info / resolve_and_install_tools / create_and_protect_remote — no consolidated prompt-sequence doc.

tests.name: upgrade_command_exits_zero
tests.details: After workaround stub of .claude/intake-progress.json (see Critical bug), `bash scripts/upgrade-project.sh --to-production </dev/null` exits 0. Output: 'Upgrade Complete', 'POC constraints removed', auto-commit 'chore(upgrade): POC -> production'. Without the workaround the script fails with `[FAIL] Project is not in POC mode.` even though phase-state.json clearly reports poc_mode='sponsored_poc'.

tests.name: phase_state_preserved
tests.details: Pre: current_phase=2, project='uat-agent-80', track='standard', deployment='organizational'. Post: current_phase=2 (unchanged), project='uat-agent-80' (unchanged), track='standard' (unchanged), deployment='organizational' (unchanged). poc_mode key was correctly removed and last_upgrade='2026-04-25' was added.

tests.name: process_state_preserved
tests.details: .build_loop unchanged across upgrade: feature='uat-feat-baseline', step=6, all 6 steps_completed preserved, started_at='2026-04-25T16:56:45Z' preserved. .claude/build-progress.json .features_completed = ['uat-feat-baseline'] preserved.

tests.name: new_required_artifacts_listed
tests.details: Upgrade output enumerates the FILES it will rewrite (.claude/phase-state.json, tool-preferences.json, CLAUDE.md, PROJECT_INTAKE.md, intake-progress.json) but does NOT list any NEW required artifacts/checks the project must now satisfy as a production scenario (e.g., pen-test report, legal review attestation, PROJECT_BIBLE.md, PRODUCT_MANIFESTO.md). It only generically advises 'Review CLAUDE.md / PROJECT_INTAKE.md Section 8 governance fields'. The post-upgrade verify-install run did surface PRODUCT_MANIFESTO.md and PROJECT_BIBLE.md as warnings, but those are pre-existing Phase-2 expectations, not production-specific deltas.

tests.name: pending_approval_sentinel_respected
tests.details: Wrote a valid sentinel via `scripts/pending-approval.sh --offer 'Pre-upgrade approval test?' --options 'A1: Proceed' 'A2: Abort' --recommendation A1` BEFORE running the upgrade. upgrade-project.sh ran to completion without ever consulting the sentinel — no warn, no refuse, no block. The sentinel file was still present after the upgrade. grep for `pending-approval` in scripts/upgrade-project.sh finds only the comment in the changelog header (line 17), no runtime check.

tests.name: upgrade_changelog_present_in_script
tests.details: scripts/upgrade-project.sh header (lines 11-19) explicitly mentions both BL-006 (2026-04-24) and BL-015 (2026-04-25) changelog entries describing what the upgrade picks up.

framework_bugs.summary: upgrade-project.sh cannot detect poc_mode after init.sh — wrong source file
framework_bugs.reproduction: Run a stock init.sh in any POC scenario (private_poc OR sponsored_poc); confirm `.claude/phase-state.json` has `poc_mode='sponsored_poc'` (or 'private_poc') and `.claude/intake-progress.json` does NOT exist. Then run `bash scripts/upgrade-project.sh --to-production`. Script prints `[FAIL] Project is not in POC mode. Use --track and/or --deployment for non-POC upgrades.` and exits 1, even though the project IS in POC mode.
framework_bugs.expected: upgrade-project.sh should read poc_mode from `.claude/phase-state.json` (where init.sh writes it) — either as a primary source or as a fallback when intake-progress.json is missing. Today it reads poc_mode ONLY from `.claude/intake-progress.json` (scripts/upgrade-project.sh:194-198), which init.sh does NOT create on the happy path.
framework_bugs.actual: Documented happy-path command `--to-production` is broken end-to-end for every project that ran init.sh. The only way past it is to manually create a stub `.claude/intake-progress.json` containing poc_mode (which is the workaround I used to complete this UAT).

documentation_gaps.gap: Upgrade output should enumerate new artifact requirements introduced by the target deployment (production: pen test, legal review, PROJECT_BIBLE.md, PRODUCT_MANIFESTO.md, etc.) rather than just listing the files it rewrote. The runbook's `new_required_artifacts_listed` assertion has no implementation in upgrade-project.sh today.
documentation_gaps.where: scripts/upgrade-project.sh — completion summary (Upgrade Complete block); RUNBOOK.md Section F row 4.

documentation_gaps.gap: The upgrade-project.sh changelog (header lines 11-19) claims BL-015 is picked up by the upgrade, but the script never actually checks for or warns about a pre-existing pending-approval sentinel. The runbook's `pending_approval_sentinel_respected` assertion has no implementation.
documentation_gaps.where: scripts/upgrade-project.sh:17 (changelog claim) vs no runtime check; RUNBOOK.md Section F row 5.

documentation_gaps.gap: Where init.sh writes poc_mode (.claude/phase-state.json) vs where upgrade-project.sh reads it (.claude/intake-progress.json) is not documented or aligned. This is the root cause of the Critical bug above.
documentation_gaps.where: scripts/init.sh (writes phase-state.json with poc_mode) vs scripts/upgrade-project.sh:194-198 (reads only from intake-progress.json).

tests.name: no_uncommitted_secrets
tests.details: grep hits in .claude/framework/hooks/known-stdlib.txt, .github/workflows/*.yml, scripts/*.sh, docs/platform-modules/*.md, docs/reference/*.md, templates/generated/*.tmpl are framework template/documentation files mentioning the WORDS password/api_key/secret/token in security-evaluation prose, not actual credentials. No real secrets in workdir. (gitleaks ran clean during the feature commit and the upgrade auto-commit.)

tests.name: log_files_consistent
tests.details: N/A — .claude/process-audit.log does not exist because no FORCE/reset events occurred during the run. Per runbook this is the expected condition.

tests.name: no_orphaned_tempfiles
tests.details: find .claude -name '*.tmp' returns nothing.

summary: Upgrade agent (sponsored_poc -> production / web / standard / typescript) found a Critical bug: scripts/upgrade-project.sh reads poc_mode only from .claude/intake-progress.json but init.sh writes it only to .claude/phase-state.json, so the documented `--to-production` happy path fails on every project until the user manually stubs intake-progress.json. Also confirmed agents 1/14 init.sh push-failure and heredoc-fragility findings, and surfaced two new High-severity gaps (upgrade output does not list new production artifact requirements; upgrade does not respect the BL-015 pending-approval sentinel).
